{"step_by_step_thinking": "Based on the provided documents, the periodontal ligament (PDL) is described as a connective tissue that consists mostly of type I collagen bands and fibroblasts. The documents consistently mention that fibroblasts play a crucial role in forming and repairing the alveolar bone and cementum, and they are specifically noted as developing the periodontal ligaments. Additionally, document [1] states that fibroblasts differentiate from cells of the dental follicle to secrete collagen and form the principal fibers of the periodontal ligament. Therefore, the predominant connective tissue cells of the periodontal ligament are fibroblasts.", "answer_choice": "B"}
